meta.title: Tooltip
meta.description: A contextual popup that displays a description for an element.

mdx:
## Overview

Tooltips offer concise, non-obtrusive guidance and supplemental information,
enhancing user understanding and interaction with UI elements.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[React-Aria Tooltip Docs](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Tooltip.html#tooltip)
[ARIA Tooltip Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tooltip/)
[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/gHbAJGfcrCv7f2bgzUQgHq/NIMBUS-Guidelines?node-id=1695-45519&m)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Placements

Tooltip placements can populate in different areas, please chose a direction
that will be most visible for the users. and the elements that may be
interacting around them. Options include bottom left, bottom center, bottom
right, top left, top center, top right, left top, left center, left bottom,
right top, right center, right bottom.

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Tooltip triggers must be focusable and hoverable in order to ensure that all
users can activate them. When displayed, the Tooltip automatically associates
the tooltip with the trigger element so that it is described by the tooltip
content.

Authors should ensure that elements with the
[role](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Roles)
tooltip are referenced through the use of
[aria-describedby](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Attributes/aria-describedby)
before or at the time the tooltip is displayed.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal" alignItems="center" marginLeft="800" padding="800" gap="400">
    <Tooltip.Root>
      <Button>Hover me</Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>This is a tooltip</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Non-text content:** Provide a text alternative (alt text or equivalent) for
  non-text content within tooltips.
- **Info and relationships:** Programmatically establish the relationship
  between a tooltip and its trigger element using ARIA attributes like
  `aria-describedby`. Assign an id to the trigger element and point
  `aria-describedby` to that id.
- **Use of color:** Avoid relying solely on color to convey information within
  tooltips.
- **Color contrast:** Ensure tooltip text meets minimum contrast ratio
  requirements. Use a contrast checking tool.
- **Keyboard accessibility:** Make tooltips triggered by keyboard focus operable
  via keyboard. Allow users to navigate to the trigger using the Tab key. Make
  the tooltip appear on focus. Prevent tooltips from trapping keyboard focus.
  Make interactive tooltip content keyboard accessible.
- **Focus visible:** Provide a visible focus indicator for the tooltip trigger
  element when it receives keyboard focus.
- **Language of page:** Programmatically determine the tooltip content's
  language. Use the `lang` attribute on the tooltip element.
- **On focus:** Prevent tooltips that appear on focus from unexpectedly changing
  the page context.
- **Labels or instructions:** Write tooltip text that is clear, concise, and
  easy to understand.
- **Name, role, value:** Ensure assistive technologies correctly interpret the
  role of the tooltip trigger element. Use semantic HTML. Use `aria-describedby`
  to associate the tooltip with the trigger.

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Use tooltips to provide brief explanations, definitions, or instructions for
interactive elements, enhancing the user experience.

### Best practices

- **Be concise:** Keep tooltip text short and to the point. Avoid lengthy
  explanations; if more detail is needed, consider a different approach like a
  modal or help documentation.
- **Provide valuable context:** Tooltips should offer information that isn't
  immediately obvious from the UI element itself. They should add value, not
  just restate what's already visible.
- **Use clear and simple language:** Avoid jargon, technical terms, or overly
  complex phrasing. Tooltips should be easily understood by all users.
- **Position tooltips appropriately:** Place tooltips so they don't obscure the
  element they're related to or other important UI elements. Consider the user's
  mouse path and avoid accidental triggers.
- **Maintain accessibility:** Includes keyboard navigation and screen reader
  compatibility. Use ARIA attributes like `aria-describedby`to link the tooltip
  to its target element.
- **Avoid overuse:** Don't use tooltips for every single UI element. Overuse can
  make the interface feel cluttered.
- **Don't rely on tooltips for essential information:** Crucial information
  (like error states) should never be exclusively conveyed through tooltips, as
  they are not always discoverable or accessible to all users. Use tooltips for
  supplementary or secondary details.

## Usage

Tooltips are a valuable tool for providing concise, contextual information, but
they should be used strategically and not as a replacement for clear and
accessible design. Think carefully about the type of information you're
presenting and choose the most appropriate UI element for the job.

### Basic usage

The recommended approach using the compound component pattern:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal" alignItems="center" marginLeft="800" padding="800" gap="400">
    <Tooltip.Root>
      <Button>Hover me</Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>This is a tooltip</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Using tooltips for icons buttons

Icons often rely on shared universal understanding. A tooltip provides a text
label that removes any ambiguity and ensures users understand the button's
function. This is especially important for less common or custom icons.

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Describe the icon's action quickly and concisely.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal" alignItems="center" marginLeft="800" padding="800" gap="400">
    <Tooltip.Root>
      <IconButton colorPalette="primary" variant="ghost" size="md">
        <Icons.Edit/>
      </IconButton>
      <Tooltip.Content>Edit name</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Do not describe the icon visually.
> - Do not over explain the action to the user.
> - Don't overlap tooltips with the object it is clarifying.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal" alignItems="center" gap="100" marginLeft="800" padding="800">
    <Tooltip.Root>
      <IconButton
        colorPalette="primary"
        variant="ghost"
        size="md"
      >
        <Icons.Edit/>
      </IconButton>
      <Tooltip.Content>
        Pencil icon that will let you edit your profile name if you like.
      </Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Placement options

Control where the tooltip appears relative to its trigger:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal" alignItems="center" marginLeft="800" padding="800" gap="400">
    <Tooltip.Root>
      <Button>Top placement</Button>
      <Tooltip.Content placement="top">Tooltip on top</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root>
      <Button>Bottom placement</Button>
      <Tooltip.Content placement="bottom">Tooltip on bottom</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root>
      <Button>Left placement</Button>
      <Tooltip.Content placement="left">Tooltip on left</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root>
      <Button>Right placement</Button>
      <Tooltip.Content placement="right">Tooltip on right</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```
